neighborhoods.name: Heritage Hunt
neighborhoods.description: Gated 55+ active-adult community with golf course, clubhouse, indoor and outdoor pools, and full activity calendar

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes and villas in age-restricted community

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Gainesville. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Virginia Oaks / Lake Manassas
neighborhoods.description: Golf-course community on Lake Manassas with single-family homes and access to private golf and tennis amenities

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes on golf course lots

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Gainesville's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Bristow / Linton Hall corridor
neighborhoods.description: Newer townhome and starter-home subdivisions along the Linton Hall Road corridor with grocery-anchored retail
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Dominion Valley / Piedmont
neighborhoods.description: Master-planned communities (Haymarket-adjacent) with golf clubs, family pools, and top-rated Prince William County schools
neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
